title: Load TSV file and print dataset statistics using R
description: Loads a TSV (tab-separated) file using R's read.delim and prints rows, columns, total cells, file size, and object memory usage.
explanation: The command uses Rscript to execute R code that reads a TSV file specified as argument, computes basic statistics (rows, columns, cells), gets file size via file.size(), and reports object memory usage via object.size(). read.delim uses tab as separator by default.
author: Marcos de Carvalho
output: Rows: 100 Cols: 10 Cells: 1000 File size: 12345 Object size: 78.2 Kb
notes: Uses read.delim with default settings (header=TRUE, sep='\t', stringsAsFactors=FALSE in R>=4.0)
For large files, consider adding options(stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
warnings: May load entire file into memory
Very large TSV files could exhaust memory
